articles.title: New GoSerpent Malware Targets Southeast Asian Governments and Diplomats for Espionage
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented malware called GoSerpent that has been put to use in cyber attacks targeting entities in Southeast Asia since late 2025 with a focus on long-term access and intelligence gathering. Russian cybersecurity company Kaspersky, which uncovered the activity in February 2026, said it was aimed at government and diplomatic entities in

articles.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
articles.summary: Four people are killed and at least 36 injured as Ukraine's two largest cities are targeted, as Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.

articles.title: Seven Malicious Vite npm Packages Use Blockchain C2 to Deliver a RAT
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a cluster of seven malicious npm packages targeting the Vite frontend tooling ecosystem as part of a software supply chain attack. The malicious package campaign, codenamed ViteVenom by Checkmarx, marks an expansion of ChainVeil, which was observed using an "unprecedented" four-tier blockchain-based command-and-control (C2) infrastructure spanning Tron,

articles.title: Armenia Detains Russian Tourist on U.S. Warrant for REvil Hacker, Lawyers Say Wrong Man
articles.summary: Armenia has held a Russian tourist named Aleksandr Ermakov in a detention center since June 28, on a U.S. extradition request for a REvil ransomware suspect named Aleksandr Ermakov. articles.title: US and Iran exchange strikes after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
articles.summary: Kuwait says an electricity and water plant is on fire after the second such attack in two days. Iran says US strikes hit an under-construction nuclear power plant.

articles.title: New EU border system tripling time at passport control, airport boss says
articles.summary: Ryanair has also warned passengers travelling to Europe this summer to prepare for extended waits.

articles.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
articles.summary: Canadian leader Mark Carney says both the US and Canada have an equal responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say are worsening wildfire conditions.

articles.summary: In April 2026, NASA’s Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer (OCHMO) initiated a working group to review updated VTE case information, additional data gathered revealing altered blood flow status within a cohort of astronauts, and discuss progress of research and clinical activities intended to mitigate the risk of VTE during spaceflight with new […]

articles.title: Most “zombie credits” locked out of new UN carbon market after China and India snub
articles.summary: Fears of a flood of environmentally-dubious carbon credits have faded, with most old projects not able to transition to new UN market The post Most “zombie credits” locked out of new UN carbon market after China and India snub appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
articles.summary: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.

articles.title: Toronto, New York and Washington top list of most polluted cities as wildfires burn
articles.summary: Canadian officials say that fire activity in Ontario is beginning to ease, with air quality expected to improve in US cities too.

articles.title: US-Iran war: UN chief calls for new diplomatic push following attacks on civilian areas
articles.summary: The UN Secretary-General remains deeply concerned by the continuing deadly military escalation between Iran and the United States, his spokesperson said on Friday as attacks destroyed key civilian and military infrastructure across the Gulf region.

articles.title: NASA’s Chandra and IXPE Study Pulsar in Lighthouse Nebula
articles.summary: This composite image, released on July 9, 2026, shows the region around a pulsar – a neutron star with a strong magnetic field that spins incredibly fast – within the Lighthouse nebula. articles.title: GoldenEyeDog Subgroup Linked to DigiCert Breach and Code-Signing Certificate Theft
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have attributed the April 2026 DigiCert security incident to a threat activity cluster dubbed CylindricalCanine. Expel, which shared technical details of the event, described the threat actor as a sub-group of GoldenEyeDog (aka APT-Q-27, Dragon Breath, and Miuuti Group), a Chinese cybercrime group known for its targeting of the gambling and gaming sectors using

articles.title: Cuban dissident Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara goes into exile in US
articles.summary: He was arrested in 2021 during Cuba's largest anti-government protests in decades and spent five years ⁠in prison.

articles.title: Hungary's president agrees to stand down after parliament backs removal
articles.summary: Tamás Sulyok, seen as an Orbán loyalist, had until Saturday night to agree to end his term.

articles.title: EU proposes slowing down cuts to carbon emissions for businesses
articles.summary: Under the proposals, the EU plans to relax its emissions trading system to give companies more time to reduce their carbon output.

articles.title: Guterres: AI must be shaped by 'all of humanity', not a handful of powers
articles.summary: UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Friday urged governments and technology companies to work together to ensure artificial intelligence (AI) benefits all countries.

articles.title: NASA Awards Facilities Support Services Contract for Ames Research Center
articles.summary: CONTRACT RELEASE NASA has selected Chugach Intelligence Solutions LLC to provide comprehensive operations, maintenance, and repair services for NASA’s Ames Research Center in California’s Silicon Valley. The Ames Facilities Support Services II contract ensures that the center’s historic and specialized facilities are properly maintained, fully operational, and capable of supporting the agency’s missions and tenant partners in the NASA Research Park. The hybrid contract has a five-year period […]

articles.title: Major emitting countries knew of climate risks decades earlier than claimed
articles.summary: Governments have known since at least the 1960s that fossil fuel use was warming the planet. They kept quiet to avoid responsibility. The post Major emitting countries knew of climate risks decades earlier than claimed appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: Ferry carrying 116 passengers sinks off Guyana coast, authorities say
articles.summary: The vessel capsized between Georgetown and Port Kaituma, with 53 people so far rescued.

articles.title: German politician resigns over surrogacy child controversy
articles.summary: Jens Spahn faced criticism over his use of a surrogate, a ban on which he previously backed.

articles.title: Second half of summer to bring chances for rain but heatwave threat persists
articles.summary: Forecasts suggest the weather could turn more changeable during late July and August but the risk of heatwaves remains.

articles.title: At least two dead in Texas floods ravaging same area where campers died
articles.summary: The same area devastated by floods last year's floods, which caused more than 130 deaths, is once again being hit.

articles.title: Baristas behind bars: Thai prisoners brew a path back to daily life
articles.summary: It looks like a regular coffee shop, albeit set up in an old red bus. Stairs lead up to a serving hatch, with a menu of drink options. A selection of jaunty coffee-related messages like “My one and only love: Americano” and “Miss you a latte,” adorn the side of the bus.

articles.title: Hunger deepens for displaced families in Sudan’s El Obeid
articles.summary: UN humanitarians working in war-torn Sudan on Friday highlighted rapidly growing needs among the more than 100,000 displaced people now sheltering in camps in the city of El Obeid.

articles.title: NASA Welcomes Mauritius as 70th Artemis Accords Signatory
articles.summary: The Republic of Mauritius has officially joined the global coalition committed to responsible space exploration, becoming the newest signatory and seventh African country to join the Artemis Accords. articles.title: Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer
articles.summary: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. Last week the company released the latest version of its flagship LLM, GPT-5.6. OpenAI says that training it against GPT-Red made the model its most robust release yet. GPT-Red automates…

articles.title: New NadMesh Botnet Hunts Exposed AI Services for Cloud Keys and Kubernetes Tokens
articles.summary: A Go botnet called NadMesh turned up in early July hunting exposed AI services, and the operator's own dashboard claims 3,811 unique AWS keys. A Shodan harvester keeps the scan queue stocked with ComfyUI, Ollama, n8n, Open WebUI, Langflow, and Gradio: the image generators, local model runners, and workflow builders that teams stand up fast and firewall late. articles.title: The Race to Field Military Autonomy Is On, Can Trusted Information Infrastructure Keep Pace?
articles.summary: Military forces are under increasing pressure to field autonomous capabilities faster than ever before. articles.title: CISA Adds Exploited SharePoint RCE Zero-Day CVE-2026-58644 to KEV
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a newly patched security flaw impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to apply the fixes by July 19, 2026. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-58644 (CVSS score: 9.8), a critical deserialization

articles.title: As blue economy gathers pace, communities must benefit from ocean boom, activists say
articles.summary: As governments turn to the ocean for jobs, growth and climate solutions, campaigners at the Our Ocean Conference in Mombasa warned coastal communities must not be left behind The post As blue economy gathers pace, communities must benefit from ocean boom, activists say appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: AI governance debate silent on risks to nature, campaigners warn
articles.summary: Global efforts to ensure AI does more good than harm must consider how it could fuel exploitation of biodiversity and ecosystems, conservationists say The post AI governance debate silent on risks to nature, campaigners warn appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: From coffee grounds to guinea pig bedding - the composters keeping it local
articles.summary: Food waste is a resource like water and should be kept in communities, compost champions say.

articles.title: Is Burnham promising a new dawn for North Sea oil and gas?
articles.summary: The incoming PM is expected to announce fresh support for the North Sea from day one of his premiership.

articles.title: Perseid meteors to light up night sky in one of the year's most active showers
articles.summary: The Perseid meteor shower will light up the night sky over the next five weeks.

articles.title: A former Ferrari boss has a blunt message: Your commute is more dangerous than his old job
articles.summary: Jean Todt spent decades around the deadliest motorsport on Earth. He was a rally co‑driver, then ran Ferrari’s Formula One team, then became president of world motorsport’s governing body. In his early years, a driver dying at the track barely made news.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Appeal for calm in Kashmir, women on the move, Ebola deaths mount, 12th anniversary of MH17
articles.summary: The UN human rights chief, Volker Türk, has appealed for calm in Pakistan-Administered Kashmir amid unrest ahead of regional elections.

articles.title: Venezuela: IOM warns of potential El Niño threat to families displaced by earthquake
articles.summary: Thousands of people remain displaced three weeks after the devastating earthquakes in Venezuela and the possible formation of a strong El Niño climate pattern could worsen their already dire situation, the International Organization for Migration (IOM) warned on Thursday.

articles.title: NASA Pushes New Wing Design to Find Structural Limits
articles.summary: NASA researchers recently put a new wing design, appearing long and thin with a lightweight structural design, through a series of grueling tests to find its structural limits. What they found left them encouraged about the wing’s potential, even when they pushed it past its intended limits. The 15-foot Structural Wing Experiment Evaluating Truss-bracing (SWEET-15) […]
